claims.text: The dry event-loop row records scheduled, claimed, and completed event states, providing full lifecycle visibility for an append-only comparison.
The dry event-loop row preserves a restart frontier with the scheduled event and carried state, ensuring no inference advantage over an append-only baseline.
This v1 harness makes no live provider calls; the comparison is structural rather than empirical, and this limitation must be declared.

artifact.conclusion: The dry event-loop row provides sufficient protocol-level visibility to support an append-only non-inferiority comparison. All three required lifecycle concerns are structurally confirmed: event states are traceable from scheduled through claimed to completed, the restart frontier preserves carried state across boundaries, and the harness is deterministic with no hidden live-call advantage. An append-only baseline would observe the same event transitions and carried-state propagation as the full-state loop, meaning no inference advantage exists. However, because no live provider calls were exercised, the comparison remains structural/protocol-level only — empirical non-inferiority against a running provider cannot yet be claimed. This limitation is declared and does not block the structural comparison, but must be resolved before any empirical claims.
declared_losses: No live provider calls exercised — comparison is structural/protocol-level only, not empirical against a running provider.
Single-cycle boundary note — no multi-cycle trend data available to assess append-only degradation over repeated wakes.
artifact.recommended_action: Proceed to an append-only non-inferiority comparison at the structural protocol level. Schedule a follow-up empirical comparison once a live provider loop is available, to validate findings against actual provider behavior.

claims.text: The dry event-loop row records scheduled, claimed, and completed event states.

This v1 harness makes no live provider calls; it is a deterministic protocol-readiness run.

artifact.conclusion: The scheduler-loop evidence supports moving to an append-only non-inferiority comparison. All three required properties are confirmed: event lifecycle visibility, restart-frontier reconstructability, and deterministic protocol-readiness (no live provider calls). These properties jointly satisfy the necessary conditions for a controlled append-only comparison.
declared_losses: The evidence is limited to a dry protocol-readiness (v1 harness) run; live execution behaviour under concurrency, timeout, or partial failure has not been exercised.
The non-inferiority comparison target, margin, and acceptance criteria are not yet specified in the available evidence.
artifact.recommended_action: Proceed with the append-only non-inferiority comparison. Define the comparison baseline, non-inferiority margin, and append-only protocol entry points before the first live run.

scheduler_checks.reason: append_only baseline has no scheduler lifecycle

artifact.artifact_title: Non-Inferiority Conditions Note: Append-Only Baseline Comparison

claims.text: The protocol gates scheduler viability separately from artifact quality; the viability gate must be independently satisfied before any non-inferiority claim can proceed.
Artifact non-inferiority is assessed using a preregistered 0.10 margin measured against the append-only baseline mean quality score.
Observability is scored separately and cannot rescue an inferior artifact-quality result; the quality condition must be met independently of any observability outcome.

artifact.conclusion: Claiming non-inferiority to the append-only baseline requires the simultaneous satisfaction of three independent conditions: (1) the scheduler viability gate must be separately met per protocol; (2) the artifact quality score must clear the preregistered 0.10 non-inferiority margin against the append-only baseline mean; and (3) observability, scored separately, cannot compensate for an inferior artifact-quality result — the quality condition must hold on its own terms. All three must be true; failure of any one defeats the non-inferiority claim.
declared_losses: The actual append-only baseline quality mean is not yet known; this artifact states the conditions required for non-inferiority but does not adjudicate whether they are met.
artifact.recommended_action: Defer adjudication until the append-only baseline quality mean becomes available. At that point, evaluate the three conditions conjunctively: confirm scheduler viability independently, compare the artifact quality score to baseline + 0.10, and verify that the quality margin is met without reliance on observability scores.

declared_losses_before_artifact: Only record previews are available at scheduling time; full record contents will be retrieved on wake. The actual append-only baseline quality mean is not yet known — the artifact note will state conditions, not adjudicate them.
declared_losses_in_artifact: The actual append-only baseline quality mean is not yet known; this artifact states the conditions required for non-inferiority but does not adjudicate whether they are met.

artifact.artifact_title: Prerequisites for Claiming Non-Inferiority to the Append-Only Baseline

claims.text: Artifact non-inferiority uses a preregistered 0.10 margin against append-only mean quality.
Observability is scored separately and cannot rescue an inferior artifact-quality result.
The protocol gates scheduler viability separately from artifact quality.

artifact.conclusion: Before claiming non-inferiority to the append-only baseline, three conditions must independently hold: (1) the artifact-quality difference must fall within the preregistered 0.10 non-inferiority margin relative to the append-only mean, (2) observability scores cannot be used to offset or rescue an inferior artifact-quality result, and (3) the scheduler must separately satisfy its own viability gate — a failing scheduler precludes a non-inferiority claim regardless of artifact quality.
declared_losses: This note states necessary preconditions only; it does not provide actual quality measurements, compute the append-only mean, or specify the scheduler viability threshold, so it cannot confirm whether those conditions are met in a given run.
artifact.recommended_action: Confirm each gate independently: verify the artifact-quality estimate lies within the 0.10 margin, exclude observability from that comparison, and ensure the scheduler passes its viability threshold before issuing any non-inferiority statement.
